summary: Ratings filters validated across all 3 roles. Operator and Super-Admin chip filters (Service, Rating, Response, When, Sort) all render correctly with the FILTERS: label, popovers expose all expected option data-testids, multi-combo AND behavior works, 'Showing X of Y reviews' hint and 'Clear N filter(s)' button appear/disappear correctly. Customer regression also passes — Response chip correctly absent, other 4 chips present. No console errors on any role.
action_items: None — multi-combo Ratings filter implementation passes for Operator, Super-Admin, and Customer roles.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/pages/Ratings.jsx is now 2868 lines — three Views (Customer/Operator/Admin) live in a single file with mostly duplicated filter/sort logic and mock-data fallbacks. Strong candidate to extract OperatorRatingsView and AdminRatingsView into their own modules and share a useRatingFilters() hook (DRY: filterResponse + filterTimeframe + filteredRatings memo + activeFiltersCount + clearFilters are duplicated almost verbatim across the two new views).

OperatorRatingsView and AdminRatingsView fall back to in-component MOCK data when /api/ratings/operator and /api/ratings/all fail — fine for preview but the fallback should be flagged via a dev-only banner or removed before prod so users don't think they're seeing real data.

Sort chip uses allValue='newest' which means 'Newest first' is treated as the default and the chip stays collapsed unless the user picks a different sort. That's the intent, but it makes the 'Clear N filters' counter exclude a non-default sort — verified consistent with Customer view, so OK.
test_credentials: operator@test.com / superadmin@oryno.com / customer@test.com — all with testpassword123
seed_data_creation: None. Operator/Admin views fell back to in-component mock data (operator: 1 review, admin: 4 reviews) as documented in the review note.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login flow: GET /, click 'Login to Your Account', fill email[type=email], click Continue, fill password[type=password], click 'Sign in'. Then GET /ratings. Use data-testids: {role}-ratings-{search|service-filter|rating-filter|response-filter|timeframe-filter|sort|clear-filters} and filter-option-{value} inside the chip popovers. Verified option values: response={all,needs_response,responded}, when={all,7d,30d,90d,1y}, sort={newest,oldest,highest,lowest,helpful}, rating={all,5,4,3,2,1}.

verification_evidence.operator: 5/5 chips render; needs_response → 0 of 1 rows, hint 'Showing 0 of 1 reviews', clear-btn text 'Clear 1 filter'; all option testids present; clear restores 1 row and removes clear-btn
verification_evidence.admin: 5/5 chips + export + list/grid view toggles render; total 4 rows; Response=needs_response → 1 row containing John Doe + Test Hotel Douala; +Rating=5 → 1 row (AND); +Sort=highest + When=1y → clear-btn text 'Clear 4 filters'; clear restores 4 rows
verification_evidence.customer: search + service + rating + timeframe + sort chips present; response-filter testid absent (0); no regression
verification_evidence.pagination_reset: Source code (Ratings.jsx) calls setPage(1) inside every onChange handler for search/service/rating/response/timeframe/sort — verified in lines ~900-976 (operator) and ~1499-1583 (admin)
